resumeLength: One page for roles; academic/policy CV may extend when research, publications, or public service justify it.

formattingRules.fontRecommendation: Clean professional font; section labels should make policy evidence easy to scan.
formattingRules.bulletStyle: Evidence-led bullets with policy object, method, and stakeholder.

writingRules.bulletFormula: Action + policy/government question + research method/source + output + stakeholder or decision value
quantificationRules: Prefer concrete scope: sample size, stakeholder count, case volume, event size, model accuracy, project duration, budget, risk, or response time when supported by source facts.
When exact numbers are absent, use bounded evidence such as tool, method, deliverable, audience, or process scope; never invent metrics.
Use metrics differently by domain: technical performance for engineering, compliance/accuracy for audit, writing/research output for law and policy, service reliability for hospitality.

exampleBullets.weak: Worked on a public policy project.
exampleBullets.strong: Analyzed municipal policy documents and stakeholder interviews to draft a 6-page brief on implementation barriers.
exampleBullets.reason: Names source materials, method, deliverable, and policy value.

exampleBullets.strong: Synthesized public records and community feedback into briefing notes for weekly department meetings.
exampleBullets.reason: Keeps the claim factual while showing audience and use.

scoringCriteria.description: Conventional headings, readable layout, aligned dates, consistent spacing, and parser-friendly text.
Prominent sections and bullets match the target role and market context.
Bullets show action, method, scope, and outcome without unsupported claims.
Specific, concise, role-native language with no inflated AI phrasing.
Structure and density match the selected role, country, and CV/resume style.

promptSnippets.title: Public Policy and Government CV routing rule
promptSnippets.content: When the target role matches Policy / Government / Public Sector, use Public Policy and Government CV as the primary industry route and adopt this formula: Action + policy/government question + research method/source + output + stakeholder or decision value.

promptSnippets.title: Public Policy and Government CV language guardrail
promptSnippets.content: Preserve domain-native evidence for Policy / Government / Public Sector; do not convert the profile into a generic tech, finance, or consulting resume unless the user target explicitly requires it.

sourceReferences.label: Local 123456 CV sample corpus
sourceReferences.note: 56 newly added CV/resume samples from docs/sample-resume-knowledge; abstracted into rules only, without copying resume text.

sourceReferences.label: Existing local resume-learning corpus
